steps.title: Find the median position for data set A
steps.content:
Data set A contains 41 values. The median is at position (41 + 1) ÷ 2 = 21. Count the cumulative frequencies: value 0 takes position 1; value 1 takes positions 2–8 (cumulative: 8); value 2 takes positions 9–16 (cumulative: 16); value 3 takes positions 17–25 (cumulative: 25).

Position 21 falls in the range 17–25, so median A = 3.

steps.title: Compare with the median of data set B
steps.content: Data set B removes the single 13, leaving 40 values. The median is the average of positions 20 and 21. Both positions still fall in the value-3 group, so median B is $(3+3)\div2=3$. Desmos can verify this by expanding the frequency table into lists for A and B. Since both medians equal 3, choice C is correct.
